headline: Dry Gas Production in Appalachian Basin Reaches Seasonal High Amid Mild Spring Conditions
body: Dry natural gas output from Appalachian Basin producers hit an estimated 35.2 Bcfd this week, reflecting improved well performance and minimal operational disruptions. Mild spring temperatures are keeping demand-side pressure subdued.

headline: Gulf Coast LNG Feedgas Nominations Rise to 14.1 Bcfd as Cargo Schedules Tighten
body: LNG export facilities along the Gulf Coast reported combined feedgas nominations of 14.1 Bcfd this week, near the operational ceiling for currently commissioned capacity. Shipping market tightness is maintaining export demand at elevated levels.

headline: Heat Dome Expected Across Southern Plains and Southeast Through Mid-July
body: The National Weather Service is forecasting a persistent upper-level ridge to maintain above-average temperatures across the southern US through at least July 18. Power-sector gas burn estimates are tracking 5–8% above the 5-year average for the period.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of 22 Bcf — Below Consensus of 34 Bcf
body: The Energy Information Administration reported a net natural gas storage injection of 22 Bcf for the week ending July 19, 2024, falling short of analyst consensus estimates near 34 Bcf. The below-consensus injection reflects persistent power-sector demand from the ongoing warm weather pattern.

headline: New Permian Basin Processing Facility Begins Operations, Adding 1.3 Bcfd Associated Gas Capacity
body: A newly commissioned natural gas processing plant in the western Permian Basin entered service this week, providing the infrastructure to handle an additional 1.3 Bcfd of associated gas production from oil-directed drilling activity in the region.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of 52 Bcf — Above Consensus of 43 Bcf; Total Reaches 3,312 Bcf
body: The Energy Information Administration reported a net injection of 52 Bcf for the week ending August 30, 2024, exceeding analyst consensus. Total Lower-48 storage stands at 3,312 Bcf, roughly 18 Bcf above the 5-year average for the date as cooling demand eases heading into the fall shoulder season.

headline: European Gas Storage at 94% Capacity; Spot LNG Demand from Asia Accelerating into Q4
body: European natural gas storage reached 94% of capacity ahead of the winter heating season, dampening near-term transatlantic LNG demand. However, Asian spot buyers are increasing activity, with cargo trading activity out of the US Gulf Coast showing a shift toward Pacific Basin delivery.

headline: Maintenance Season Concluded; Major Northeast Pipeline Capacity Fully Restored Ahead of Winter
body: Annual fall maintenance work on a key Northeast natural gas pipeline corridor was completed on schedule, with full system capacity restored ahead of the winter heating season. Flow nominations are rising as forecasters begin flagging early-season cold weather risk.

headline: Arctic Outbreak Forecast for Late November Across Midcontinent and Northeast
body: A significant polar vortex displacement is expected to bring temperatures 10–16°F below normal across the Midwest and Northeast for the week beginning November 25. Heating demand models suggest the event could draw down storage inventories by 180–230 Bcf if sustained.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -214 Bcf — Largest Single-Week Draw Since February 2021
body: The Energy Information Administration reported a net withdrawal of 214 Bcf for the week ending November 29, 2024, the largest single-week storage draw in nearly four years. Total storage fell to 3,098 Bcf, now 62 Bcf below the 5-year average as the late-November cold event drove heating demand well above seasonal norms.

headline: Appalachian Basin Producers Report Minimal Freeze-Off Activity Despite Cold Temperatures
body: Major natural gas producers in the Appalachian Basin reported limited wellhead disruptions during the late November cold event, citing improvements in cold-weather preparation protocols and heated production infrastructure. Dry gas output remained within 1.5% of pre-event levels throughout the period.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -188 Bcf — Second Consecutive Above-Average Winter Draw
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 188 Bcf for the week ending January 3, 2025. Total storage now stands at 2,641 Bcf, 148 Bcf below the 5-year average for the date. The back-to-back large draws are reflecting sustained below-normal temperatures across the eastern half of the country.

headline: FERC Approves Capacity Expansion on Appalachian-to-Midwest Pipeline Corridor
body: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission granted approval for a capacity expansion project that would increase natural gas transport capacity from the Appalachian Basin into Midwest markets by approximately 0.8 Bcfd. The project is targeted for completion in Q3 2026.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -162 Bcf — Storage Deficit Widens to 210 Bcf Below 5-Year Average
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 162 Bcf for the week ending February 7, 2025. Total storage stands at 2,312 Bcf, now 210 Bcf below the 5-year average for the date and approaching the lower bound of the 5-year historical range.

headline: Temperature Pattern Moderating; Above-Normal Warmth Forecast Through Late March Across Eastern US
body: The Climate Prediction Center is forecasting above-normal probabilities for temperatures across the Eastern US for the 30-day outlook period ending late March. The moderation in winter heating demand is expected to allow storage withdrawals to ease toward seasonal norms.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -28 Bcf — End-of-Season Inventory at 1,748 Bcf
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 28 Bcf for the week ending March 21, 2025. End-of-withdrawal-season storage stands at 1,748 Bcf, 185 Bcf below the 5-year average — a significant deficit entering the injection season that analysts expect to persist through early summer.

headline: Haynesville Shale Rig Count Increases for Fourth Consecutive Month; Operators Respond to Stronger Price Signal
body: The active rig count in the Haynesville Shale formation in Louisiana and East Texas increased for the fourth consecutive month in March, with several operators citing the improvement in realized prices since the winter as the primary driver for reinstating previously deferred completions.

headline: New Gulf Coast LNG Train Enters Pre-Commissioning Phase; Commercial Operations Expected Q3 2025
body: A new liquefaction train at a Gulf Coast LNG export facility entered the pre-commissioning phase this week. The operator expects to achieve first LNG production by late August, with commercial operations commencing in Q3 2025. When operational, the train adds approximately 0.6 Bcfd to US export capacity.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of +96 Bcf — Deficit vs. 5-Year Average Narrows for First Time in 12 Weeks
body: The EIA reported a net injection of 96 Bcf for the week ending May 23, 2025, above analyst consensus and the largest weekly build since August 2024. The storage deficit vs. the 5-year average narrowed to 168 Bcf as production growth and mild temperatures support refilling.

headline: EPA Finalizes Methane Emissions Rule for Upstream Oil and Gas; Industry Groups Assess Compliance Costs
body: The Environmental Protection Agency finalized a new methane emissions standard for upstream oil and gas operations, requiring enhanced leak detection and monitoring protocols at well pads and compressor stations. Industry associations have indicated the rule will require significant capital investment over a 3-to-5-year compliance window.

headline: Prolonged Heat Wave Across Southwest and South Central US; Power Grid Stress Testing Demand Records
body: An upper-level heat dome has locked in extreme temperatures across the Southwest and South Central US, with multiple grid operators reporting peak summer demand records. Natural gas fired generation is providing the margin resource, with power-sector burn estimated at 42 Bcfd — roughly 4.5 Bcfd above the 5-year average for mid-July.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of +18 Bcf — Below Consensus of +31 Bcf as Power Demand Persists
body: The EIA reported a net injection of only 18 Bcf for the week ending July 25, 2025, well below consensus as sustained summer power demand limits storage refilling. The cumulative injection-season shortfall relative to the 5-year average has widened to 112 Bcf entering August.

headline: New Gulf Coast LNG Train Achieves First Commercial Cargo; Export Capacity Lifted by 0.6 Bcfd
body: A recently completed liquefaction train loaded its first commercial LNG cargo, officially adding approximately 0.6 Bcfd of export capacity to the US system. Feedgas nominations at the facility are expected to ramp to plateau operations within 45 days.

headline: Gulf of Mexico Platform Restoration Complete After Storm Disruption; Offshore Production Returns to Pre-Event Levels
body: Operators in the deepwater Gulf of Mexico confirmed that production had been fully restored following the temporary shut-in associated with a tropical weather system that passed through the region in late August. The total disruption amounted to approximately 0.5 Bcfd for roughly 6 days.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of +71 Bcf; Total Storage at 3,481 Bcf, 99 Bcf Below 5-Year Average
body: The EIA reported a net injection of 71 Bcf for the week ending September 26, 2025. Total storage stands at 3,481 Bcf, 99 Bcf below the 5-year average for the date, as the injection season draws toward its conclusion. End-of-season storage is now projected at 3,520–3,580 Bcf.

headline: DOE Issues LNG Export Authorization for Additional Capacity at Brownfield Gulf Coast Terminal
body: The Department of Energy issued a long-term export authorization for an additional 1.2 Bcfd of LNG export capacity at an existing Gulf Coast terminal. The authorization is contingent on completion of a capacity expansion project expected to begin construction in Q1 2026.

headline: Cold Pattern Building: La Niña Influence Expected to Dominate Winter 2025–2026 Temperature Outlook
body: The Climate Prediction Center's winter outlook shows elevated probability of below-normal temperatures across the northern tier of the US for December–February, consistent with a La Niña pattern that has strengthened over the past six weeks. The outlook suggests increased heating demand relative to last winter.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -48 Bcf — Withdrawal Season Opens Below 5-Year Average Inventory
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 48 Bcf for the week ending November 14, 2025, marking the official start of the withdrawal season. Total storage stands at 3,531 Bcf, now 70 Bcf below the 5-year average after a below-pace injection season. The deficit heading into winter is a notable feature of this season's setup.

headline: Associated Gas Production Growth from Permian Basin Continues to Offset Haynesville Decline
body: Analysts tracking dry gas production note that growth in associated natural gas from the Permian Basin is partially offsetting a modest decline in Haynesville Shale volumes. Net national dry gas production remains near all-time highs at approximately 103 Bcfd.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -171 Bcf — Early Season Cold Accelerates Inventory Decline
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 171 Bcf for the week ending December 12, 2025, well above the 5-year average draw of 118 Bcf for the date. Total storage has fallen to 2,849 Bcf, now 122 Bcf below the 5-year average as early-season cold weather has delivered above-average heating demand.

headline: Polar Vortex Disruption Forecast for Second and Third Weeks of January; Widespread Below-Normal Temperatures Expected
body: A major polar vortex displacement is expected to push a persistent Arctic air mass across the central and eastern US from January 11 through approximately January 24. The NWS is forecasting temperatures 12–20°F below normal across the Midwest, Ohio Valley, and Northeast for a 10-to-14-day stretch.

headline: Transco Zone 6 Basis Spikes to Multi-Year Highs as Northeast Heating Demand Surges
body: Natural gas basis differentials at key Northeast delivery points reached multi-year highs this week as prolonged below-normal temperatures drive heating demand to extreme levels. Pipeline constraints are creating significant price separation between regional markets and Henry Hub.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -268 Bcf — Largest Single-Week Draw in Over a Decade
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 268 Bcf for the week ending January 16, 2026, the largest single-week storage draw since 2014. Total storage stands at 2,283 Bcf, now 212 Bcf below the 5-year average. The deficit relative to year-ago levels has widened to 318 Bcf.

headline: Minor Appalachian Freeze-Off Activity Reported; Production Recovers Within 48 Hours
body: Several smaller producers in the Appalachian Basin reported temporary wellhead freeze-offs during the coldest period of the January polar vortex event. Affected output is estimated at 0.8 Bcfd at peak impact; production returned to near-normal levels within 48 hours as temperatures moderated.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -149 Bcf; Cumulative Season Draw Pacing 18% Above 5-Year Average
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 149 Bcf for the week ending February 13, 2026. Cumulative withdrawal-season draws are now running 18% above the 5-year average, consistent with the higher-than-normal heating demand delivered so far this winter. Total storage stands at 1,842 Bcf.

headline: Senate Energy Committee Holds Hearings on LNG Export Permitting Reform
body: The Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee convened hearings to examine proposed changes to the LNG export permitting process, including potential modifications to the public interest determination standard used by the Department of Energy. Industry and environmental groups presented conflicting testimony on the appropriate role of export policy in domestic price formation.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Withdrawal of -31 Bcf — End of Season Approaching; Inventory at 1,748 Bcf
body: The EIA reported a net withdrawal of 31 Bcf for the week ending March 13, 2026, suggesting the withdrawal season is nearing its end. Total storage stands at 1,748 Bcf, 179 Bcf below the 5-year average — a more pronounced end-of-season deficit than the comparable period in 2025.

headline: Spring Temperature Outlook: Equal Chances Across Most of US; No Strong Signal for Late-Season Cold
body: The Climate Prediction Center's April–June temperature outlook shows equal chance probabilities across most of the eastern and central US, providing no strong signal for late-season heating demand events. The neutral outlook reduces the near-term probability of additional storage deficit widening from the demand side.

headline: Haynesville Shale Operator Announces 8-Well Pad Completion Campaign Targeting Late Q2 Production
body: A major Haynesville Shale operator announced commencement of an 8-well pad completion campaign, with first production from the pad expected in late June 2026. The company cited the improvement in the winter forward price curve as the primary economic driver for the accelerated completion schedule.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of +18 Bcf — Injection Season Begins With Deficit at 185 Bcf
body: The EIA reported a net injection of 18 Bcf for the week ending April 4, 2026, confirming the beginning of the injection season. Total storage stands at 1,766 Bcf, 185 Bcf below the 5-year average for the date. Analysts note the deficit is larger than the start of the 2025 injection season.

headline: Mountain West Compressor Station Returns to Service After Extended Repair Period
body: A key natural gas compressor station on a major Mountain West pipeline corridor resumed full operations after a 6-week repair period following equipment failure. The restoration returns approximately 0.4 Bcfd of Rockies-to-Midwest transport capacity to service.

headline: European Buyers Begin Early LNG Procurement for 2026–2027 Winter; Spot Activity Elevated
body: European energy traders are reporting early engagement in the spot LNG market, with activity for Q4 2026 and Q1 2027 delivery showing elevated pricing and tight availability. Market participants attribute the front-loading to concerns about storage refilling adequacy following a colder-than-normal 2025–2026 winter.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of +52 Bcf — Below Consensus of +68 Bcf; Deficit Persists at 140 Bcf
body: The EIA reported a net injection of 52 Bcf for the week ending April 18, 2026, falling short of consensus estimates for the third consecutive week. Total storage stands at 1,818 Bcf, 140 Bcf below the 5-year average. The persistent below-consensus injections suggest ongoing demand pressure from LNG exports and power generation.

headline: FERC Issues Final Rule on Pipeline Rate Reform; Tariff Transparency Requirements Expand
body: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission issued a final rule requiring expanded transparency in interstate natural gas pipeline tariff filings, including more granular reporting of operational flow constraints and available firm capacity. The rule takes effect 90 days after publication in the Federal Register.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of +48 Bcf — Fourth Consecutive Below-Consensus Build
body: The EIA reported a net injection of 48 Bcf for the week ending April 25, 2026, below consensus estimates of +62 Bcf and representing the fourth consecutive week of below-consensus injections. Total storage stands at 1,866 Bcf, maintaining the deficit vs. the 5-year average near 138 Bcf.

headline: Dry Gas Production Edges Higher as DUC Inventory Draws Down in Marcellus; Haynesville Completions Ramp
body: National dry gas production has increased modestly in recent weeks, driven by the completion of drilled-but-uncompleted wells in the Marcellus Shale and accelerated completions in the Haynesville. The incremental volumes are estimated at 0.5–0.8 Bcfd above January 2026 levels.

headline: Early Cooling Season Forecast: June–August Temperature Outlook Skews Warm Across Southeast and South Central
body: The Climate Prediction Center's June–August outlook shows above-normal temperature probabilities across the Southeast and South Central regions, consistent with a developing warm phase that could support above-average power-sector gas demand through the summer injection season.

headline: EIA Storage Report: Net Injection of +43 Bcf — Deficit vs. 5-Year Average Holds Near 138 Bcf
body: The EIA reported a net injection of 43 Bcf for the week ending May 2, 2026. Total storage stands at 1,909 Bcf. The storage deficit vs. the 5-year average held near 138 Bcf, with no meaningful narrowing in the most recent week. LNG export demand and above-seasonal power-sector gas consumption are cited as the primary factors limiting injection-season progress.

headline: US LNG Export Feedgas Demand Reaches New Monthly High in April; Asia Premium Over TTF Widens
body: US LNG export feedgas demand averaged 14.8 Bcfd in April 2026, setting a new monthly record and reflecting strong cargo loading activity driven by the widening of the Asia LNG price premium over the European Title Transfer Facility benchmark. Analysts note the trend is incrementally supportive for Henry Hub prices heading into summer.

headline: Morning Update: NGM26 Volatility Elevated; Front-Month Basis Structure Suggests Near-Term Supply Tightness
body: Front-month Henry Hub prices opened with elevated intraday volatility this morning, consistent with the current elevated volatility regime. The basis structure across near-month contracts reads as consistent with near-term supply tightness relative to demand, as storage refilling pace remains below seasonal norms for the fourth consecutive week.
